English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Edward Bowditch papers
Bowditch, Edward, 1881-
4292
1.7 cubic feet. 1.7 cubic feet.
[ca. 1907-1957] Typescripts, manuscripts, printed pamphlets, letters and memos, photographs, negatives, and photograph albums. Bound volumes include typescripts, manuscripts, printed pamphlets, letters and memos on various aspects of Philippine history and life. Specific volumes concern irrigation; copies of responses to a survey of tribes in the Department of Mindanao; and correspondence between Bowditch and W. Cameron Forbes, with biographical information about Americans who served in the Philippines. Also five photograph albums, mostly identified, of people and places in the Philippines; loose photographs of the Philippines; and negatives. Collection material in English 2084418
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
E. Engst
Date completed:
June 1987
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, May 2003
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
After receiving a law degree from Harvard University, Edward (Peter) Bowditch came to the Philippines in 1907 and became Secretary to the Governor General, and Secretary and Vice Governor of Moro Province, a position he held until 1914 when it was transferred from military to civil control. Bowditch then served with the first civil governor of Moro Province for a period of six months. During World War I, he served in France, becoming personal A.D.C. to General Pershing. He was a member of the Harbord Mission to Armenia in 1919, served as an officer in the 5th and 12th Cavalry regiments, and returned to the Philippines as a member of the Wood-Forbes Mission in 1921.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Bound volumes include typescripts, manuscripts, printed pamphlets, letters and memos on various aspects of Philippine history and life. Specific volumes concern irrigation; copies of responses to a survey of tribes in the Department of Mindanao; and correspondence between Bowditch and W. Cameron Forbes, with biographical information about Americans who served in the Philippines. Also five photograph albums, mostly identified, of people and places in the Philippines; loose photographs of the Philippines; and negatives.
Cite As:
Edward Bowditch papers, #4292. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Mindanao Island (Philippines) Philippines -- Politics and government -- 1898-1935. Philippines -- History -- 1898-1946. Philippines -- Description and travel -- Views. Philippines -- American influences. Irrigation -- Philippines. Photograph albums Forbes, W. Cameron (William Cameron), 1870-1959.
2084418 <<<<<<< HEAD Bound volume of reports, notes, etc. on irrigation, compiled ca.Vol. 119081Copy of responses to a survey of tribes in the Dept. of MinanaoVol. 21
Scope and Contents
Subjects include original and natural phenomena; government; laws; marriage and divorce; prostitution, abortion, and infanticide; funeral rites; religion; superstitions; slavery, education; warfare; the chase and fishing; manners and customs; dress; games; music and dancing; commerce; self mutilation of the body; uses of medicine and methods produced in childbirth; folklore and tradition; history; and languageMindaneo papers - Spanish and Army - typescripts of various reports, compiledVol. 319141The Brethren - sequel to the Apostles -Vol. 71956-19571Correspondence of Bowditch and W. Cameron Forbes concerning people who were involved in the government of the Philippines; includes biographical information on the staffVol. 71Lists of words and expressions in Moro, and additional survey reports on Moros and other tribes on Minanao (see Vol. 2)Vol. 42Includes "Special Banquet Number" of the Merchants' Association Review, 1911; typescript message of the Governor General to the Second Philippine Legislature, 1911; Address to the Merchants' Association, 1911; various press releases relating to conditions in the Philippines; memos and reports about the economic and agricultural conditions, 1908, 1913; report from the Court of Land Registration relating to simplification of land titles; reports on "The New Mentality of the Filipino" and on "Modern Japan"; and published "Diary of a Journey Among the Lois of Hainan, Feb. and Mar. 1910," by J. de L. Simonds. Also, loose clippings; letters about a testimonial to Forbes, 1930; and a 1961 letter to Bowditch with an excerpt from a diary entry by ForbesVol. 5191119081913193019612Report by the Nationalist Party of the Secretary of War; correspondence about a child support case, 1909; trial transcript and report relating to Mariano Noriel, Philippine insurgent; report on the Philippine constitution, 1910; typescript translation of newspaper reports on the deportation of Chinese, 1909; and speech on the inauguration of Governor Carpenter, the first civil governor of Mindanao,Vol. 619091910190919142Loose photographs of the Philippines, unidentifiedVol. 62List of photos from a trip north of Luzon,Vol. 619112Photograph album, Philippines, 1907-1909, identified1907-19093Photograph album, Philippines, 1908-1909, identified1908-19094Photograph album, Philippines, some commercial views,1914-19155Photograph album, Philippines, n.d., unidentifiedn.d.5Photograph album, Philippines, ca. 1913, identified19135Negatives (nitrate)6 ======= Bound volume of reports, notes, etc. on irrigation, compiled ca.Vol. 119081Copy of responses to a survey of tribes in the Dept. of MinanaoVol. 21
Scope and Contents
Subjects include original and natural phenomena; government; laws; marriage and divorce; prostitution, abortion, and infanticide; funeral rites; religion; superstitions; slavery, education; warfare; the chase and fishing; manners and customs; dress; games; music and dancing; commerce; self mutilation of the body; uses of medicine and methods produced in childbirth; folklore and tradition; history; and languageMindaneo papers - Spanish and Army - typescripts of various reports, compiledVol. 319141The Brethren - sequel to the Apostles -Vol. 71956-19571Correspondence of Bowditch and W. Cameron Forbes concerning people who were involved in the government of the Philippines; includes biographical information on the staffVol. 71Lists of words and expressions in Moro, and additional survey reports on Moros and other tribes on Minanao (see Vol. 2)Vol. 42Includes "Special Banquet Number" of the Merchants' Association Review, 1911; typescript message of the Governor General to the Second Philippine Legislature, 1911; Address to the Merchants' Association, 1911; various press releases relating to conditions in the Philippines; memos and reports about the economic and agricultural conditions, 1908, 1913; report from the Court of Land Registration relating to simplification of land titles; reports on "The New Mentality of the Filipino" and on "Modern Japan"; and published "Diary of a Journey Among the Lois of Hainan, Feb. and Mar. 1910," by J. de L. Simonds.
